title: The Kitten Who Found a Friend
moral: A true friend sees the special light inside you and helps it shine brighter — even when you can't see it yourself.
moralZh: 真正的朋友能看到你内心独特的光芒，并帮助它更加闪耀——即使你自己都看不见。

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: A little orange kitten named Coco had just moved to a new neighborhood with her family. Everything was different — the streets, the trees, the sounds. And worst of all, she didn't have any friends yet.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: One afternoon, Coco sat on her front porch, watching other animal children play across the street. They were laughing and chasing each other. Coco's tail drooped.

paragraphs.speaker: Mama Cat
paragraphs.text: "Why don't you go say hello, sweetie?"

paragraphs.speaker: Coco
paragraphs.text: "I'm too nervous, Mama. What if they don't like me?"

paragraphs.speaker: Mama Cat
paragraphs.text: "You'll never know until you try. Just be yourself — that's more than enough."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Coco took a deep breath and padded down the street. But as she passed the old oak tree, she heard a strange sound — a soft sniffle. She peeked behind the tree and saw a small bunny with floppy ears, sitting alone with a paintbrush in her paw.

paragraphs.speaker: Coco
paragraphs.text: "Hi... are you okay?"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The bunny looked up, startled. Her big round eyes were shiny with tears.

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "Oh! I — um — I'm fine!"

paragraphs.speaker: Coco
paragraphs.text: "You don't look fine. My name is Coco. I just moved here. What's your name?"

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "I'm Pip. I... I was painting, but I messed up. It's ruined."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Coco looked at the painting lying on the grass. It was a painting of a sunset over a meadow — and even to Coco's untrained eye, it was absolutely beautiful. But there was one tiny wobble of orange paint near the bottom.

paragraphs.speaker: Coco
paragraphs.text: "Ruined? Pip, this is AMAZING! Did you really paint this?"

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "It's not that good... See this messy spot? I was trying to paint a butterfly, but my paw slipped."

paragraphs.speaker: Coco
paragraphs.text: "Wait! Don't paint over it. That orange blob... it looks like the sun is saying hello! It makes the painting even more special."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Pip blinked. She had never thought of it that way before. A tiny smile crept onto her face.

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "You really think so?"

paragraphs.speaker: Coco
paragraphs.text: "I know so! Pip, you are SO talented! Do you have more paintings?"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Pip's ears perked up. She hesitated for a moment, then nodded shyly. She pulled out a worn sketchbook from her bag, filled with painting after painting — fields of wildflowers, a moonlit river, a rainbow over the hills.

paragraphs.speaker: Coco
paragraphs.text: "Pip! These are marvelous! You should show everyone!"

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "Oh no, no, no! I could never. I'm too shy. What if they laugh at me?"

paragraphs.speaker: Coco
paragraphs.text: "Then I'll be right there with you. Friends don't let friends hide their masterpieces."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The next day, Coco and Pip set up a little art gallery under the old oak tree. Coco hung Pip's paintings on a string between two branches and called all the neighborhood children to come see.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: At first, Pip wanted to hide behind the tree. But Coco held her paw and whispered, "You've got this." When the other animals gathered around and said "Whoa!" and "Did you paint these?" — Pip's heart swelled with joy.

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "Do they really like them?"

paragraphs.speaker: Coco
paragraphs.text: "They LOVE them! I told you! See? Your art is your superpower."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: That evening, as the sun set and the two new friends sat under the oak tree, Coco looked around at their new neighborhood — and it didn't feel so strange anymore.

paragraphs.speaker: Pip
paragraphs.text: "Coco? Thank you for seeing something in me that I couldn't see myself. You're the best friend I've ever had."

paragraphs.speaker: Coco
paragraphs.text: "And you're the best friend I've ever had in this whole new neighborhood. You know what they say... some friends are born when two hearts bump into each other at just the right time."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Coco was right. She had come looking for friends, but instead she found something even better — she found someone whose light she could help shine. And that, after all, is what true friendship is all about.
